arts.ttl: Aston Villa 'reject' £40m Jhon Duran bid from Premier League rival amid Barcelona, PSG interest
arts.sum: Aston Villa are reportedly fending off interest for Jhon Duran from West Ham United, who have seen a £40m bid for the Colombian striker rejected.

arts.ttl: Barcelona star has twice as many assists as any other midfielder in the club’s squad this season

arts.ttl: ‘Everthing has changed’ - Hansi Flick talks battle for Barcelona No. 1 spot between Iñaki Peña and Wojciech Szczesny
arts.sum: The goalkeeper situation at Barcelona has certainly become very interesting since the beginning of the new year, as Wojciech Szczesny has started the last three games and conceded just one goal in place of Iñaki Peña. Szczesny had to wait a long time for his chance since coming out of retirement to join the club in September, but the Pole has certainly showed he has what it takes to be the club’s starting shot-stopper for the rest of the season.

arts.ttl: Laporta defends Barcelona's handling of Olmo situation, says club was under attack
arts.sum: Laporta said in a news conference that a portion of the media and opposition members wanted the club to fail in its bid to register the Spain midfielder, who is back after Barcelona won a government ruling. He thanked clubs such as Real Madrid for not taking a public stance against Barça’s attempt to register the players.

arts.ttl: Barcelona president says club hounded ‘by land, air and sea’ over Olmo saga
arts.sum: Joan Laporta says enemies hoped to ‘destabilise’ the club President defends ‘contained euphoria’ before Super Cup Joan Laporta denounced an attack on Barcelona “by land, air and sea” and insisted there was no reason why he should resign as he spoke publicly for the first time since the club were allowed to register Dani Olmo and Pau Víctor in defiance of the ruling by La Liga and the country’s football federation. The Barcelona president claimed the club had correctly presented “all” the documentation needed to register Olmo and Víctor by the 31 December deadline but admitted they had not been able to provide the proof of income to comply with financial controls until 3 January. He alleged that was through no fault of their own and that the league made unexpected and unnecessary demands of Barcelona, hinting that the federation (RFEF) had said the registration should have been completed.
